acquisition_method: run python worldcup_data_audit/scripts/build_non_odds_context_inputs.py to fetch https://api.fifa.com/api/v3/rankings/?gender=1&count=300&language=en and write processed/fifa_ranking_snapshot.csv
notes: Used as a small national-team strength prior alongside Elo; raw JSON cached at worldcup_data_audit/data/raw/fifa_rankings_men_latest.json.

acquisition_method: run python worldcup_data_audit/scripts/pull_and_audit_worldcup_data.py to rebuild processed/statsbomb_event_half_team_summary.csv from StatsBomb event JSON; EventAgent reads it automatically
notes: Predicts yellow cards, red cards, corners, free kicks, and penalties by first half / second half. Current sample is partial World Cup open data, not a full provider feed.

acquisition_method: run python worldcup_data_audit/scripts/build_non_odds_context_inputs.py to align available stadium samples by match_id; refresh with exact Open-Meteo forecasts close to kickoff
notes: Generated rows reuse current weather samples and emit warnings because they are not exact kickoff forecasts.

acquisition_method: run python worldcup_data_audit/scripts/build_non_odds_context_inputs.py to derive travel_km/timezone_shift/rest_days from group-stage fixture order and stadium coordinates
notes: Team base camps are not available; first group match is neutral and knockout travel is not pre-generated.

acquisition_method: run python worldcup_data_audit/scripts/build_non_odds_context_inputs.py to generate 48 team style priors; replace with pre-match scouting/provider export when available
notes: Model treats 50 as neutral; current values are heuristic priors rather than confirmed coaching plans.

acquisition_method: run python worldcup_data_audit/scripts/build_non_odds_context_inputs.py to generate a global World Cup event-environment prior; replace with assigned referee history when known
notes: Current row is not an assigned referee; it is a global cards/fouls/penalty prior.

acquisition_method: run python worldcup_data_audit/scripts/build_non_odds_context_inputs.py to copy StatsBomb player-match sample into model contract; full club/national feed should replace it
license_or_access: StatsBomb attribution for open data; licensed feeds vary
notes: Partial national-team sample; useful as a working model input but not full coverage.

acquisition_method: write official starters, substitutes, unavailable players, expected minutes, and status into lineup CSV
notes: Starters require official=true unless --allow-projected-lineups is used.

acquisition_method: use normalized FC26 ratings as optional fallback when lineup rating is missing
notes: Game ratings only; not FIFA federation official ratings and not live form.
